threats.title: JWT Private Key Served at Public HTTP Endpoint
threats.scenario: GET /encryptionkeys/jwt.pem served by Express static middleware at server.ts:277-278 with no authentication. Any HTTP client can download the RSA private key and use it to forge admin JWTs.
threats.controls_in_place: None — publicly accessible static file serving

steps: If public key must be accessible for JWKS, serve only jwt.pub — never jwt.pem

threats.title: HTTP Access Logs Publicly Readable
threats.scenario: GET /support/logs/ served publicly at server.ts:281-283. Logs contain HTTP requests including Authorization headers, email addresses, and OAuth tokens passed as query parameters (T-008). Any visitor can download attack history.

steps: Add security.isAuthorized() and isAdmin() before /support/logs routes
Forward logs to external SIEM instead of serving from application

threats.title: XXE via XML File Upload
threats.scenario: File upload at server.ts:309 passes to handleXmlUpload which uses libxmljs2. If external entity resolution not disabled, attacker uploads malicious XML with file:///etc/passwd or file:///juice-shop/encryptionkeys/jwt.pem entity reference to read arbitrary files.

steps: Pass {noent: false, nonet: true} options to libxmljs2.parseXmlString()
Verify no XXE expansion in all XML parsing code paths

threats.title: FTP Directory Publicly Browsable
threats.scenario: GET /ftp/ served with directory listing at server.ts:269. Contains challenge files, legal.md, and potentially sensitive documents. Enables unauthenticated file download of all FTP contents.
threats.controls_in_place: Intentional CTF design; quarantine subdirectory exists

steps: In production deployment: require authentication for /ftp/ routes
Remove sensitive files from FTP directory in non-CTF deployments
